FastAPI asserts that 204 status codes must not have a response body.

and evaluation_schedule routers prevents this assertion in CI.

* fix: return Response(204) directly instead of decorator status_code

FastAPI 0.104.1 asserts that 204 status codes must not have a response
body at route registration time, even with response_class=Response.
Return Response(status_code=204) from the handler instead.

commit.message:
feat: 7-layer anti-tamper protection (SIGTERM handlers, sentinel supervisor, cross-monitoring)

L1: Watchdog SIGTERM handler — synchronous alert before dying on bootout
L2: Guardian monitors watchdog process — alerts if watchdog killed
L3: Watchdog heartbeat file — guardian detects stale heartbeat with boot grace
L4: Sentinel daemon — re-bootstraps watchdog if killed via bootout
L5: Sentinel SIGTERM handler — alerts before sentinel dies
L6: Watchdog cross-monitors sentinel — re-bootstraps if sentinel killed
L7: Sentinel emergency guardian spawn — last resort if both watchdog + guardian dead

Shutdown vs attack: guardian monitors watchdog, so system shutdown (both die)
never triggers false alarms. Boot grace periods (3-4 min) prevent startup noise.

commit.message:
Hearth: interactive actions for Needs Attention beads (Forge-rx8) (#60)

* feat: Hearth interactive actions for Needs Attention beads (Forge-rx8)

Add action menu overlay when pressing Enter on a selected bead in the
Needs Attention panel. Available actions:

- Retry: clears needs_human flag and resets retry count, putting bead
  back in the dispatch queue
- Dismiss: removes the retry record so the bead leaves the panel
- View Logs: shows the last 50 lines from the most recent worker log
  in a scrollable overlay

Implementation spans five layers:
- ipc: new RetryBeadPayload, DismissBeadPayload, ViewLogsPayload types
- state/db: ResetRetry, DismissRetry, LastWorkerLogPath methods
- daemon: IPC handlers for retry_bead, dismiss_bead, view_logs commands
- hearth: action menu popup, log viewer overlay, placeOverlay helper,
  status flash messages in footer
- cmd/forge/hearth: wires IPC callbacks (OnRetryBead, OnDismissBead,
  OnViewLogs) following the existing OnKill pattern

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-rx8

- Add EventRetryReset and EventBeadDismissed event types; use them in
  retry_bead and dismiss_bead handlers instead of EventClarificationCleared
- Replace os.ReadFile tail-read with seek-from-end approach to avoid
  loading entire log files into memory for view_logs handler
- Fix placeOverlay to use lipgloss.Width() for ANSI-aware overlay width
  measurement and afterOverlay position calculation
- Fix renderLogViewer line truncation to use rune slicing with bounds check
- Add tests for ResetRetry, DismissRetry, LastWorkerLogPath in db_test.go
- Add tests for retry_bead, dismiss_bead, view_logs IPC handlers in daemon_test.go

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve CI failures for Forge-rx8

retry_bead IPC handler now detects circuit-breaker state (dispatch_failures > 0)
and calls ResetDispatchFailures instead of ResetRetry, returning "circuit breaker reset".
For beads with needs_human set by non-circuit-breaker reasons, retry_bead returns "ok"
without modifying state. Updates TestHandleIPC_RetryBead/success to test circuit-breaker path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-rx8

- ResetRetry: also reset dispatch_failures to 0 so the circuit breaker
  does not immediately re-trip after a manual retry
- TestDB_ResetRetry: set DispatchFailures=3 on insert and assert it is
  cleared to 0 after reset
- ipc.go: remove extraneous blank line after ViewLogsResponse

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix team generation for all batch sizes with two-pass approach

Problem:
- Some batches (like CVE-2027 with 46 students) have no valid
  team configuration where total teams are divisible by 3
- This is mathematically impossible for certain student counts

Solution - Two-pass algorithm:
1. First pass: Try to find teams divisible by 3 (preferred)
2. Second pass: If that fails, allow ANY valid team configuration
   - e.g., 10 teams instead of 12, if that's only option

Benefits:
✓ Handles all batch sizes
✓ Still prefers divisible-by-3 when possible
✓ Falls back gracefully for edge cases
✓ Better error handling and logging

Updated version to v31

P0: remove ib_v9 path — both endpoints now use build_prompt() from instruction_builder.py

Non-streaming endpoint was calling ib_v9.build() which had NONE of the P0 fixes
(no language auto-detection, no dialect prohibition, no confusion escalation).

commit.message:
feat(agent-detail): dynamic request detail page + LATERAL JOIN + scalability indexes

- Agent detail: single LATERAL JOIN query (service_request + user + payment) replaces 5 sequential queries
- Agent checklist: dynamic extraction from Python workflow classes when DB config is empty
- List query: CTE with COUNT(*) OVER() window + indexed ORDER BY created_at DESC (was COALESCE non-indexable)
- Fix days_since bug: was subtracting same date (always 0), now uses date.today()
- Migration 180: 4 composite indexes for 1M+ scale (workflow+created, assigned+workflow+created, workflow+status+created, history request+performed)
- Frontend: 3 data classifications (meta badges, identity highlighted, complementary), payment/tariff cards
- i18n: 25 document names + payment/tariff keys in es/fr/en
- Fix namespace: DocumentosTab/TraitementTab → agent.requestDetail
- Fix hardcoded Spanish labels → getDocName() i18n with snakeToTitle fallback

commit.message:
fix: race conditions, silent errors, and HiDPI text in GPU renderer

- Queue texture uploads before GL init, flush on initializeGL
- Guard rerenderHiRes against concurrent async page renders
- Block rendering on shader compilation/link failure
- Validate GL context before makeCurrent in uploadTexture
- Render text labels at physical pixel size for HiDPI sharpness
- Remove no-op mirrored(false,false) deep copy on every upload
- Rename m_pdfRenderTimer to m_hiResRenderTimer (used for both)

commit.message:
fix(daemon): prevent dev-reload deadlock and ensure resource monitor starts

Two fixes:

1. daemon.py:start() — early return when 0 workers skipped ALL background
   tasks including the resource monitor. Restructured so only pilot init
   is conditional; background tasks (resource monitor, heartbeat, usage,
   conflicts, mtime watcher) always start regardless of worker count.

2. daemon.py:run_daemon() — os.execv on dev-reload inherited the flock
   fd, so the new process image couldn't acquire the lock and died with
   "Another daemon already running". Extracted _exec_restart() helper
   that closes the lock fd before exec.

Also fixes dashboard.js field names for _fmtGB() and
updateResourcePopover() to match the server's resource payload.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
